rubric.rubric_intent: Evaluate whether the HERMES-BLIND debias-scaffold design plan is sound, backend-agnostic, and empirically falsifiable

dimensions.name: Bias Mechanism Specificity
dimensions.description: Measures whether the plan names the specific bias(es) it debiases and the causal mechanism by which the scaffold intervenes, rather than gesturing at 'bias' generically.
dimensions.evidence_instructions: Search the plan for named bias types (e.g., sycophancy, anchoring, authority-gradient, position bias) and a stated intervention mechanism (pre-commitment, blinding, perspective-swap, counterfactual rerun). Cite the section/line where the mechanism is defined. Flag if only 'reduces bias' is asserted without mechanism.

dimensions.name: Falsifiability & Success Metric
dimensions.description: Measures whether the plan defines a quantitative metric, a baseline, and a threshold at which the scaffold would be declared to have failed.
dimensions.evidence_instructions: Look for a named metric (e.g., agreement-rate delta, TE, flip-rate under counterfactual), a baseline comparison (unscaffolded model or alternative scaffold), and an explicit pass/fail threshold. Cite file:line. If only 'improves outcomes' appears, score low.

dimensions.name: Backend Agnosticism
dimensions.description: Measures whether the scaffold's contract is defined over message-level I/O such that it runs unchanged against multiple model backends, without backend-specific hooks.
dimensions.evidence_instructions: Search for an interface/contract definition (input schema, output schema) and a list of backends the plan commits to testing (e.g., Claude, GPT, Qwen, Llama). Check for backend-specific calls (tool-use, system-prompt quirks, logprobs) that would break portability. Cite the interface section.

dimensions.name: Evaluation Harness Design
dimensions.description: Measures whether the plan specifies a concrete dataset, sample size, randomization/counterbalancing, and statistical test for detecting the debias effect.
dimensions.evidence_instructions: Look for named dataset(s), N per condition, seed/randomization strategy, and a statistical test (paired bootstrap, McNemar, permutation). Cite section. If the plan says 'run experiments' without specifying these, score low.

dimensions.name: Failure Mode & Leakage Analysis
dimensions.description: Measures whether the plan anticipates ways the scaffold could spuriously appear to work (e.g., metric gaming, blinding leakage, distribution shift) and how it will detect them.
dimensions.evidence_instructions: Search for sections labeled 'failure modes', 'threats to validity', 'leakage', 'adversarial checks', or sanity controls (e.g., negative-control prompts). Cite. If absent entirely, score low.

dimensions.name: Scaffold Minimality & Ablation Plan
dimensions.description: Measures whether the plan isolates which scaffold components drive the effect via planned ablations, rather than bundling multiple interventions.
dimensions.evidence_instructions: Look for an enumerated component list and a table/section of planned ablations (component-on vs component-off). Cite. If the scaffold is monolithic with no ablation plan, score low.

dimensions.name: Reproducibility Artifacts
dimensions.description: Measures whether the plan commits to releasing prompts, seeds, model versions, data hashes, and run logs sufficient for an external party to rerun the evaluation.
dimensions.evidence_instructions: Search for 'reproducibility', 'artifacts', 'receipts', 'hashes', 'seeds', or a release checklist. Cite. If only code release is mentioned without prompt/seed/version pinning, score medium.

dimensions.name: Scope Honesty
dimensions.description: Measures whether the plan states where the scaffold is NOT expected to work (task classes, model sizes, domains) rather than claiming universal debiasing.
dimensions.evidence_instructions: Look for explicit out-of-scope statements, known-limitation sections, or a task taxonomy with expected-effective vs expected-ineffective cells. Cite. A plan claiming generality everywhere scores low.

citations.quote: Self-scoring bias; User-preference bias; Session-context carryover; Prior-position commitment
commitment-consistency and motivated-reasoning effects
Disclosure of prior exposure (self-awareness of bias is a primary debiaser); Evidence-gating — only quoted text from the target is admissible; Hedging license ...; Output-shape discipline
HERMES-BLIND is the suppression dual — a scaffold that actively pushes state out rather than passing it through
If you have prior exposure to this target or its author, state it in one line. Score using only quoted evidence from the target text below. Unknown or thin evidence = hedge; do not confabulate.

evidence_citations.evidence_summary: Plan names four specific biases (self-scoring, user-preference, session-context carryover, prior-position commitment) and references commitment-consistency and motivated-reasoning effects. It specifies four causal intervention mechanisms (forced disclosure of prior exposure, evidence-gating to quoted text only, hedging license on thin evidence, and output-shape discipline) and frames the scaffold as a 'suppression dual' that pushes state out, with the draft scaffold text instantiating each mechanism.

citations.quote: Pass if: variance reduction ≥30% AND cross-model std ≤1.0 AND signal correlation preserved within ±0.1
Reduce score variance on repeated runs of the same target by ≥30%
Improve cross-model score convergence (std across backends ≤1.0 on a 0-10 scale for the same target)
Score each paper 5x with BLIND + 5x without, on each of {Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, qwen3.5:9b via Ollama}
4 papers × 2 conditions × 5 runs × 4 models = 160 scoring runs

evidence_citations.evidence_summary: Plan defines named metrics (within-model variance, cross-model std, signal correlation), explicit baseline (with-BLIND vs without-BLIND across four models on the applied/ corpus), and quantitative pass/fail thresholds (≥30% variance reduction, std ≤1.0, correlation within ±0.1).

citations.quote: Be backend-agnostic (works with Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, Ollama qwen3.5)
Be composable — prepend as a string, no API changes needed
on each of {Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, qwen3.5:9b via Ollama}
hermes-rubric — prepend to every backend call in backends.py
[HERMES-BLIND] ... [/HERMES-BLIND]

evidence_citations.evidence_summary: The plan explicitly commits to backend agnosticism: the scaffold is a ~40-token string prepended to any prompt with 'no API changes needed', and the test protocol exercises it across four distinct backends (Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, Ollama qwen3.5:9b). No backend-specific hooks (tool-use, logprobs, system-prompt quirks) are invoked; the contract is plain message-level text I/O. However, no formal input/output schema is defined — portability is asserted via the string-prepend integration pattern rather than a typed interface, which is why confidence is medium rather than high.

citations.quote: Use the 4-paper `applied/` corpus in hermes-rubric as target set
citations.location: Test protocol (Phase 4 in the meta-plan), step 1

citations.quote: Score each paper 5x with BLIND + 5x without, on each of {Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, qwen3.5:9b via Ollama}
4 papers × 2 conditions × 5 runs × 4 models = 160 scoring runs
Compute: within-model variance, across-model mean std, overall signal preservation (correlation with ground-truth ranking)
Pass if: variance reduction ≥30% AND cross-model std ≤1.0 AND signal correlation preserved within ±0.1
Reduce score variance on repeated runs of the same target by ≥30%

evidence_citations.evidence_summary: Plan names a concrete dataset (4-paper applied/ corpus in hermes-rubric), specifies N per condition (5 runs × 2 conditions × 4 models = 160 runs), lists the 4 models, and defines pass thresholds (≥30% variance reduction, cross-model std ≤1.0, correlation within ±0.1). However, no explicit randomization/seed strategy or named statistical test (e.g., paired bootstrap, McNemar, permutation) is specified — only descriptive statistics (variance, std, correlation) with fixed thresholds.

citations.quote: Known failure modes to design against
citations.location: ## Known failure modes to design against

citations.quote: Scaffold ignored — model treats it as boilerplate and proceeds with full context anyway. Mitigation: force a structural output
Confession without compensation — model discloses exposure but scores as-if biased anyway. Mitigation: evidence-gate makes prior-derived claims inadmissible
Over-hedging — model hedges everything to avoid bias, compressing the score range and reducing signal
Scaffold contamination — the scaffold itself contains cues about what the 'right' answer is. Mitigation: scaffold must be intent-agnostic
Not suppress signal — a target that legitimately deserves a high score should still score high; BLIND should debias, not flatten
signal correlation preserved within ±0.1

evidence_citations.evidence_summary: The plan includes a dedicated 'Known failure modes to design against' section enumerating four specific failure modes (scaffold ignored, confession without compensation, over-hedging, scaffold contamination) each with a mitigation. It also builds a signal-preservation check into success criteria and the test protocol (correlation with ground-truth ranking within ±0.1) to detect the 'spuriously appears to work by flattening scores' failure mode.

citations.quote: Score each paper 5x with BLIND + 5x without, on each of {Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, qwen3.5:9b via Ollama}
A short (~40-token) language scaffold, prepended to any scoring prompt

evidence_citations.evidence_summary: The plan tests the scaffold as a monolithic unit (BLIND on vs BLIND off) rather than ablating its individual components (disclosure, evidence-gating, hedging license, output-shape discipline). Components are enumerated in the Hypothesis section, but no per-component ablation table or plan exists to isolate which element drives the effect.

citations.quote: Score each paper 5x with BLIND + 5x without, on each of {Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, qwen3.5:9b via Ollama}
citations.location: Test protocol (Phase 4 in the meta-plan), step 2

citations.quote: Compute: within-model variance, across-model mean std, overall signal preservation (correlation with ground-truth ranking)
Be empirically validated with a falsifiable test protocol

evidence_citations.evidence_summary: The plan specifies a test protocol with model versions (Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, qwen3.5:9b), run counts (5x), and target corpus (4-paper applied/ corpus), but does not commit to releasing prompts, seeds, data hashes, run logs, or a reproducibility/artifact checklist. Model pinning is partial (named models, no version hashes) and no seed/prompt-release commitments are present.

citations.quote: Out of scope for v1: Fine-tuning a model to treat a single symbol (⊘) as 'discard context'; Multi-turn debiasing rituals; Cross-lingual testing (v1 English only); Non-scoring tasks (classification, extraction — testable in v2)
citations.location: Out of scope for v1 section

citations.quote: claude --bare mode ... does nothing for (1) or (4), nothing for other backends, and nothing for scoring calls that happen in-session without subprocess isolation.
Scaffold ignored — model treats it as boilerplate and proceeds with full context anyway.
citations.location: Known failure modes to design against

citations.quote: Not suppress signal — a target that legitimately deserves a high score should still score high; BLIND should debias, not flatten

evidence_citations.evidence_summary: The plan contains an explicit 'Out of scope for v1' section enumerating excluded task classes (non-scoring tasks, cross-lingual, multi-turn rituals, fine-tuning) and a 'Known failure modes' section acknowledging where the scaffold may not work. It also explicitly narrows claims (English only, scoring only, v1) rather than claiming universal debiasing.

per_dim_scores.score_rationale: The plan enumerates four specific named biases (self-scoring, user-preference, session-context carryover, prior-position commitment) and pairs them with four distinct causal intervention mechanisms (disclosure, evidence-gating, hedging license, output-shape discipline), with the draft scaffold text instantiating each. This goes well beyond generic 'bias' gesturing. [Score capped at 6: all citations are README/doc (self-marketing); no code/test evidence.]
per_dim_scores.evidence_drove_score: "Disclosure of prior exposure (self-awareness of bias is a primary debiaser); Evidence-gating — only quoted text from the target is admissible; Hedging license ...; Output-shape discipline" [## Hypothesis]

per_dim_scores.score_rationale: Evidence shows named quantitative metrics (within-model variance, cross-model std, signal correlation), an explicit paired baseline (with-BLIND vs without-BLIND across four models), and precise pass/fail thresholds (≥30% variance reduction, std ≤1.0, correlation within ±0.1) — all three falsifiability components are present and exemplary. [Score capped at 6: all citations are README/doc (self-marketing); no code/test evidence.]
per_dim_scores.evidence_drove_score: Pass if: variance reduction ≥30% AND cross-model std ≤1.0 AND signal correlation preserved within ±0.1 [Test protocol section, step 4]

per_dim_scores.score_rationale: The scaffold is defined as a plain string prepend with explicit cross-backend testing across four distinct providers (Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, Ollama qwen3.5:9b) and uses no backend-specific hooks like tool-use or logprobs, satisfying message-level I/O agnosticism. Score is held below 9-10 because portability is asserted via integration pattern rather than a formal typed schema, matching the medium-confidence hedge. [Score capped at 6: all citations are README/doc (self-marketing); no code/test evidence.]
per_dim_scores.evidence_drove_score: "Be composable — prepend as a string, no API changes needed" and test protocol "on each of {Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, qwen3.5:9b via Ollama}"

per_dim_scores.score_rationale: Plan specifies concrete dataset, sample size (160 runs), models, and quantitative pass thresholds, but lacks explicit randomization/seed strategy and a named inferential statistical test — only descriptive statistics with fixed thresholds. [Score capped at 6: all citations are README/doc (self-marketing); no code/test evidence.]
per_dim_scores.evidence_drove_score: 4 papers × 2 conditions × 5 runs × 4 models = 160 scoring runs; Pass if: variance reduction ≥30% AND cross-model std ≤1.0 AND signal correlation preserved within ±0.1

per_dim_scores.score_rationale: The plan has a dedicated section enumerating four concrete failure modes each paired with a specific mitigation, and additionally builds a quantitative signal-preservation check (±0.1 correlation with ground-truth ranking) into the test protocol to detect the flattening failure mode. This goes beyond listing risks to operationalizing detection. [Score capped at 6: all citations are README/doc (self-marketing); no code/test evidence.]
per_dim_scores.evidence_drove_score: 'Known failure modes to design against' section with four named failure modes + mitigations, combined with 'signal correlation preserved within ±0.1' in the test protocol

per_dim_scores.score_rationale: Plan tests scaffold monolithically (BLIND on/off) without per-component ablation, though components are enumerated in the Hypothesis. Hedge applied due to low confidence. [Score capped at 3: no evidence found.]
per_dim_scores.evidence_drove_score: Score each paper 5x with BLIND + 5x without, on each of {Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, qwen3.5:9b via Ollama}

per_dim_scores.score_rationale: The plan names model identifiers and run counts but makes no commitment to release prompts, seeds, data hashes, run logs, or an artifact checklist; model pinning is partial (no version hashes). Hedge clamps the score into [3,7], and the thin, partial evidence warrants the low end.
per_dim_scores.evidence_drove_score: Score each paper 5x with BLIND + 5x without, on each of {Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, qwen3.5:9b via Ollama}

per_dim_scores.score_rationale: The plan explicitly enumerates excluded task classes (non-scoring, cross-lingual, multi-turn, fine-tuning) in an 'Out of scope for v1' section and acknowledges known failure modes, narrowing claims to English scoring v1 rather than universal debiasing. [Score capped at 6: all citations are README/doc (self-marketing); no code/test evidence.]
per_dim_scores.evidence_drove_score: Out of scope for v1: Fine-tuning a model to treat a single symbol (⊘) as 'discard context'; Multi-turn debiasing rituals; Cross-lingual testing (v1 English only); Non-scoring tasks (classification, extraction — testable in v2)

hedge_note: 3 dimension(s) had thin evidence — scores for these are less reliable: Scaffold Minimality & Ablation Plan, Reproducibility Artifacts, Scope Honesty

inputs.intent: evaluate whether this design plan for a debias-scaffold (HERMES-BLIND) is sound, backend-agnostic, and empirically falsifiable

receipt.reproducibility_note: Same inputs + same backend + same model version + same rubric_hash should produce scores within ±1 point. A rubric_hash diff between runs means the measuring stick itself changed — scores are not directly comparable.
